title: excoecaria biglandulosa var. lanceolata
intro: Excoecaria biglandulosa var. lanceolata, also known as the lance-leaf Excoecaria, is a tropical plant species native to the Pacific Islands, belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. The plant's leaves and roots possess medicinal properties, primarily containing toxic compounds such as phorbol esters and flavonoids, which have shown potential in treating various health conditions. The lance-leaf Excoecaria is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, providing health benefits such as wound healing, skin protection, and the management of fever and digestive issues. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used in preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, although caution is advised due to its toxic nature, and proper preparation and dosing are essential to harness its medicinal benefits safely.
